reviews.review_msg: Friendly and looks very smart, explain stuff well and at the end he asks you to explain it back to him.  ask him whats on the exam and he ll give you some helpful hints
This guy is awsome
Avoid this Guy. he told me Ill be fine and I got F

312 is a killer course but he tries to make it easier. Fashionable guy, doenst answer emails. ended up with B+. dont take it summer time as the course is very demanding. Ace the first midterm , final will screw you.
Nice Guy, Knows his stuff, smart, provide his phone number to students but never picks up the phone!

Absolutely awful teacher. Bahram did not answer emails and when I went to his office hours once and did not show up. He also canceled his only office hours a day before the final. The Course was entirely run by TAs and the prof only did an hour of reading slides out loud and prepared tests unrelated to assignments. one of the worst profs at sfu

Bahram is a super nice guy but has 0 teaching ability. During the online semester, he just pre-recorded videos of himself reading Pearson slide decks word for word and did nothing else. Exams featured questions never seen before on homework/textbook. Stopped providing practice questions after the first exam. Class was an absolute waste of money.

The prof read off the slide and did a few easy example questions that were already given to you on Canvas. We had quizzes every week that also came straight from same answer key we already had. Gave little information about midterm and final and the difficulty level was way harder than practice questions from each chapter.
Vague marking rubrics. Unclear on instructions. Poor, unhelpful feedback. Pretty passive aggressive. Would not recommend.

I understand that this is his first time teaching, butttt, wth is that. Who teaches like that, he would constantly make mistakes in calculations. He went over 4 chapters on the second lecture. Super dry, but was accommodating. I truly belive that he should read the slides before explaining them to us, and I thing that SFU sucks at picking profs.

Altug is a solid prof and is passionate about finance as a subject. He will make sure to help his students achieve academic success, all you have to do is take the time out and ask and attend his office hours. He prefers to solve questions by hand in class which is very helpful in preparing for exams. His grading was clear as well.

This was Altug's first time teaching and I thought he did a pretty decent job. He was very flexible and accommodating to his students requests and ensured that our learning was his focus. The lectures were really dry as he just read right off of them. Basically, you got to learn the content on your own, but his assignments weren't overly difficult.

You're in good hands with Jose. He excels at "improv" (answering questions, spontaneous examples, etc). When he reads off the slides it can be boring, but he tries engaging the class. If it's not clear the first time, ask him, he does a good job clarifying. I read the textbook but slides were sufficient. Know the formulas and do his practice sets!!
Really easy going guy. Get yourself prepared by doing all the problem set+reading the slides will just bring you above the average! Real gem

Frederick's online class is honestly pretty easy. Practice questions in the textbook will 100% show up on the tests, but this means the averages are REALLY high (1 had 90% ave). He does 8 "mini-tests" rather than a midterm and final. However, this means that there will be one almost every week along with homework, so there's a lot to do every week.

I took the course online so could change when in person, no midterm or final, 8 weekly quizzes worth 10% each and 20% for hw. The difficulty level fluctuates depending on the chapter. However, averages were pretty high (70%-90%). To succeed, go over the textbook and practice a lot, ask questions if you need to and you should be fine.

Fred is an extremely nice man and taught many things that were outside of the textbook but in my opinion were more useful. My one complaint is that he relied heavily on connect to the point that either the test questions were just the same as the practice questions or ones that no one has ever seen before, therefore being too difficult.
He is a caring man, makes finance sound like an everyday thing. Lots of homework so be prepared for that and a high curve.

Waste of time, energy & money. Frederick did not teach; he outsourced the marking to McGraw. No midterms or final exams, just weekly homework and mini-tests. The averages were astronomically high on these assignments as all answers were on Chegg. If you want to be rewarded for studying and not cheating then do NOT take this class with Frederick.
This dude is the goat. Sweetest and most caring mans.

He is a very nice teacher and helps you in understanding the concepts. The two gripes I have are his tangents that arent relevant (sometimes), and the tests were inconsistent in difficultly. The online class are set up pretty consistently. A minitest every week. If you study a day before you'll probably be fine. Go over the textbook questions.

He is a nice enough person but as an instructor, there are much better finance professors at SFU. I don't believe his background is in finance but it is in physics and philosophy. He covers a marginal amount of material in class and expects us to learn the rest on our own. It should be taught by someone else who has a background in finance.

In this course you basically teach yourself. All tests/hw are done through a third party website. You are required to complete HW before the lecture so you basically learn even before you attend the lecture. He probably goes over 2-3 concepts in lectures, rest teach yourself. Don't know why I'm paying $$$$ to teach myself!
Really nice guy.  Go to him for help if you need it!

Yuriy's teaching style is very straightforward and clear, which I enjoyed. He doesn't waste your time and gets straight to the main points. His grading system is fair and allows you compensate your grade a bit if you bomb one of the quizzes or exams. BUS 312 is definitely a tough class with difficult tests, but I am glad I took it with Yuriy.
great TA. very helpful and always available for help. definitely recommend seeing her if you have any questions!

He will let you know directly how to do good in his class with all the material provided. He provides all of his past quizzes/midterms/finals on his website, open book/note, be prepare to practice hard and you will get what you deserve. Everything is given to you and the rest is up to you if you work hard. TAs are super helpful too

George is super smart but not so good at teaching. He rambles so much and on top of that he's got a grandpa voice that makes you want to fall asleep. However, he posts all his past exams on his website which was really helpful in studying. Aim for the highest grade u can get before the final to cushion the damage

Do the sample Midterm and Quiz. The sample is similar to the actual one. Cheating sheets are allowed. However, the final is really hard and it is totally different from the sample. Assignments are easy to get the mark. Try to get a higher mark as possible before the final.
Blazenko is an amazing prof. However, the material from the midterm to the final seemed a bit rushed. He is super funny.
Just work on past exams you will be ok. Solutions are accessible on homework. Dont need to go to classes since all lectures are recorded.

Blazenko is amazing. He gives you all of his past exams and quizzes, and there is no need to buy a textbook. For the midterm and quizzes, practicing old exams and quizzes is enough. Also, try to pace yourself for the exams, otherwise, you will not finish writing. Lastly, ask your TAs for help if needed, and do not fall behind!

George is the best prof I've had at SFU. He gives you every resource you need to be successful, and his passion for finance shows. There's might be easier profs out there, but if you're a finance major, definitely take this class with George as it will prepare you well for 315.
Amazing Prof who will go above and beyond if you go to office hours and show interest. I started a business later and he was willing to be part of the advising team to help us do well!
Live streams lectures and providers past tests for practice. Makes you work for it but youll learn a lot. Would recommend.

You really have to work hard and understand how to do the questions by practicing a lot. Sample quizzes & midterms were very similar to the actual ones. I got decently above average for both. The final was tougher but I did about 8 practice finals and ended up doing fine in the course. Just work hard and make good formula/cheat sheets for exams.
Probably my favourite in SFU
He makes everything very straight forward and has a good sense of humour. Wants his students to do well.

At first he would seem like a jerk but he is really a nice guy! He is helpful and wants his students to do well. He has that no student left behind policy :). He is a young fellow and explains concepts really well. Ya, the lectures were a bit boring but just read the book, pay attention in class and you would do just fine. i would do him ;-)

He comes off cold - however, he is actually a very nice guy. For instance, he brought Mars bars to the final exam for student in case they were hungry during the test. How many profs do that?? It seems as if he wants students to do well - on their own. Overall he is a good prof, and I would definitely take a class with him again.

The course content itself isn't hard but it's really easy to fall behind and do bad on the topic tests. I personally liked the course content but everything is self-teaching. Lectures you basically go to meet friends but learn nothing. Averages are also pretty higher than prev semesters, so before
Fully expect to teach yourself from the textbook. Terrible professor

shows up to class late, talks about her personal life way too much adds an extra hour to each lecture, lectures so simplistic and don't really help at all, no syllabus going into the third week of class..... just do your job pretty simple

Four tests and only highest three will count. Love this. Many office hours during the week to help us. Sometimes the tests have vague expressions, hope can be clearer for further students! Tara is very nice and caring for students. The only thing is she is not very responsible for Emails, so better to go office hours!

worst prof OF ALL TIME. i don't say that lightly. she has been kind to me in general but she would be better off working in a profession that doesn't involve time management or organization. i saw her a handful of times because she was straight up never in class, got TAs to teach the whole thing. exams were not relevant to course material.

Tara is extremely unorganized. We did not get a syllabus until the second week. She has tons of TAs that are just incompetent. TAs recorded solution videos that she posted on Canvas, but not only they're hard to understand, but their solutions are wrong. How can you be a TA when you can't solve problems yourself? 
This is just outrageous.

I took BUS 312 with Professor. Freda. She gave us lectures that are informative and very practical that we can apply to real world. Even though she was sometimes late for her lectures (NOT ALWAYS AS OTHERS MENTIONED), I loved her lectures and her enthusiasm towards all of her lectures. If you do all hw and attend classes, you will do well !

HORRIBLE PROF! She was late for every class. 20 min late for the midterm. 2 hours late for the final exam. Lectures were uninformative. Didnt explain anything in depth. DO NOT TAKE ANY CLASSES WITH HER!

She came 20 minutes late to every class and was always complaining that we are behind. Her notes consist of summarizing the textbook and her writing was beyond messy. Quiz 1 was incorrect, a student sent quiz to the finance department & they said it is unsolvable. She came late to both the midterm by 15 mins & final by 1.5 hours. STAY AWAY!
Although this semester doesn't went very smoothly but she gave out the grade very generous.
The worst course ever! And the worst prof ever!

The final exam was supposed to start at 12pm. It is currently 1:20pm. Freda isn't here yet and the exam hasn't started. Absolutely no word on what is happening and it's a 50% final exam. Says a lot about how the prof is I believe.

She was an hour and a half late for the final, rushes through the notes, saying "this is common sense I need to move on, there's no time" yet she comes 15 minutes late to every class, and that's being generous. She only hand writes her notes with her terrible writing. Most disorganized class I've ever been in. DO NOT TAKE!

It's 30 mins into lecture and she still hasn't shown up. She is late for every lecture, and then blames the class for being behind. Her lectures consist of rewriting the textbook in note form rather than actually working through relevant problems.

Not helpful at all. She uses another prof's textbook, assignment questions, and practice quiz yet she told us that she had a different style of testing anyways. She doesn't give the right formulas for assignments yet says the class material is super easy. She shows up late to class and then says we're behind in learning the material.

Opened each lecture with "I know, I know.  I don't want to be here either so let's just get this over with."  Lectures involved her writing down the text on overheads word-for-word.  What a waste of time!
worst prof ever.
If the class can only get 50%, this can only mean that she CAN'T teach.

What are you all complaining about. Its open book, open notes, questions are exactly the same as homework. I and others found her to be funny and entertaining. what does it matter if average grade on exam is 50% you still end up with a good mark.

Got an A- .  Like most profs, gotta play by her rules.  She doesn't like e-mails... so I use the TA office hours instead.   Otherwise, read the text before attending her lectures.  She can be nice to people who she knows put in the effort.
If she stops being so mean to students, I will like her a bit.
No Comments
Couldn't teach a dog to sit-Thinks she's god-Taunts class about test difficulty
Irrelevant, ridiculous exams. I have 3.7gpa, worked very hard and still got a C-
can't teach, rude to students, unfair exams, average less than 50%
WORST prof ever. Admitted that she only wants finance majors in her class.
Easily the worst SFU prof; snaps at students; gives outrageously unfair quizes
Worst prof I have ever had.  Can't teach, won't admit to mistakes
Don't take her. If u do, cough a lot in class.
I got an A in this class and I still think she was a poor lecturer.
Totally unfair exams! Nothing like lectures, assignments, or quizzes!
Her tests are SO unfair
she is bit better than Daniel Smith who is teaching bus 315
No time to learn...too busy taking down notes
Terrible. Just rewrites the textbook on an overhead and calls it teaching.
Gives unfair exams and she's not student friendly
TERRIBLE avoid if possible
Awful.  Will treat you like a 3 year old.  Adds nothing to text material.
Class avg 50% . . . Simply can't teach.
She's the worst prof I've ever met.
She's got attitude - can never admit to a mistake
Her teachings has nothing to do with the exams.
No Comments
She is as good as an overhead projector
tons of mistakes, hard to read her writing, doesn't teach - regurgitates info
Always makes hard exam and say its easy, class average is 50%
Rushes throughout her lectures.
No Comments

No lecture notes posted, and the textbook is very difficult to read so attend class. He explains the material very well. The midterm and the final has one tough question but the prof lets you know which ch its gonna come from. He pulls some question from the textbook practice problems. Practice Practice Practice and you'll do alright.

No lecture slides and the exams were challenging. Try to understand the concepts and examples he taught in lectures. He is very nice in general. He will answer your questions after class. In addition, questions are always encouraged during class, he will repeat some difficult calculations several times to make sure you can understand. Good prof!
he doesn't know how to teach

He's a good teacher,  but very old fashioned. Wastes 30 min of class handing quizzes back one by one, instead of giving them to TAs. No slides, only lecture notes in his horrible handwriting, so pay attention to what he says, he likes to use acronyms without writing what they actually are. Nice guy overall, wouldn't take a class with him again tho.
Don't skip class because he does not provide lecture notes. Make sure you pay attention in class and take good notes, since you'll need them to study
Don't skip class because he does not provide lecture notes. Make sure you pay attention in class and take good notes, since you'll need them to study. Also do tons of practice questions.

Ron Zitron was extremely helpful and clear. The midterms and assignments mostly reflect what he discusses in class. As there is no textbooks for this class, it is a good idea to attend all lecture as you will need his notes for assignments. To do well, YOU HAVE TO DO A LOT OF PRACTICE QUESTIONS. Midterms, assignments, exercises etc.

One of the best prof. You don't need the textbook, only have to attend every class and take notes by yourself. Quizzes are super easy, but midterm and final are super difficult. However, this class is curved, so you will be fine if you do your best to be above the average :D
Not recommended
He cares about the students and he explains the topics very well

I thought that Vincenti was a great professor. He gave very clear guidelines for the course and straightforwardly explained everything. I would suggest reading the textbook chapters before class and using his lectures to clarify any confusion. If you read the text and do all the practice problems you can easily do well in his class.

nice guy for the most part. accent makes it hard to understand, and the material is dry. quizzes are a joke, as approx 30% of the class cheats every week. told prof, and he said "if we saw someone cheating we'd take action". pretty garbage class, marked on barely anything, few opportunities to learn/practice.

He's a prof that cares about his students and learning, but his exams are on the harder end. Doing the problem sets and practice midterms/finals is not enough to do well because the real exams will have wording or incomplete information that will throw you off and really test your problem-solving ability. Overall, though, he is a good guy.

Genuinely one of the best profs I've ever had in my 3 years of school. Just a great human being, and clearly cares deeply about student success. Exams were pretty easy and cover exactly what is in the lectures and practice material. Class consisted of two optional midterms and a final (if you skip a midterm weight gets shifted to final).

Gherardo is a great professor, and is good at explaining topics relatively simply in a way students can understand, make sure to do the questions given in class, attend lectures, and go to office hours to succeed as examinations require you to understand the topics not just memorize concepts, overall a very useful class! Would recommend!

Genuinely one of the best profs I've ever had. Just a great guy and clearly cares deeply about student success. Nothing on his exams were a surprise at all—as long as you do all the practice material, you will be more than prepared. No homework, just two optional midterms and a final (if you skip a midterm weight gets adjusted to final).
very good professor, final was bit tough but he has great humanity that lot of professors lack

He is definitely one of the best prof I have ever met in sfu, there are three exams and he gives you the choice to skip or take depending on your readiness. 
The slides are straightforward and the professor provides extra practice paper, and he is super friendly to assist. Recommend.

I'm echoing the last comment: I don't do these reviews, but I feel like I have to here. The guy is great, and if you read some bad comments today, it's because it was the day of the exam and some people clearly never went to class nor to an office hour. Gherado couldn't really be more transparent about what he wants students to know. Best prof ever

Go to classes and you will be fine. Not only he teaches well, but he also makes very very clear what he wants in the exam. Both Midterms and the final I sat down and found EXACTLY what I was expecting.
I usually don't post here, but I feel like I have to given what I'm reading. The guy is incredibly good: caring, super knowledgeable, and totally available. Hands down best prof at Beedie.

He is so good. He's always incredibly willing to help: just think that the weeks before the exams he has office hours every day. It's actually one of the first classes in which I feel like I learned something, and I enjoyed every single class. I wish all professors at Beedie were like him.

He is great ! Just go to class ( or office hours - he is always happy to reexplain) and do his practice questions, and the exam is basically super easy. People complaining here probably never went to a class ! I wish all professors were like him !
I do not know what to say, probably I need to retake this course again next term. The school's arrangement is suck, there is only 1 option -- Him to take for this course...

He is a good person, but his exams are super hard. Lots of surprise questions, and it is good for students who concentrated in Finance. But really no need to make that hard for student to choose others.
Worst Lecturer I have ever had at Beedie. Avoid at all cost.
He is so terrible. The final exam was extremely hard, avoid please
50 formulas and no formula sheet, even though the prof was good, the course structure is shit, would recommend not taking this course till the structure changes
Gherardo was a great professor and he really cares about his students. He wants you to do well and always answers questions and gives guidance. Would highly recommend you take him.

He is super caring and wants his students to succeed. He is open to answer any questions students have about the content. 25% for each of the two midterms and 50% for the final. All exams were super fair and heavily reflected his problem sets, lectures, and mock exams. Attending the lectures and doing all his provided practice is enough to succeed.

Great prof. cares for his students, always available to help and answers emails quickly. exams are hard but fair despite being worth 100% of your grade. do the homework and pay attention and you'll do fine.

Gherardo was not too bad; he cared for his students. You were only examinable by two midterms worth 25 percent and 50 percent on the final, and could skip the midterm to convert the weight to the final. His lectures were easy to zone out as it could become quite dry and his exam was way harder than the practice.
I enjoyed his teaching style, and by attending each lecture, I could fully understand the concepts.

Gherardo mostly lectures from slides and tries to engage students, but participation is low so it's easy to zone-out. Slides are concise and contain enough information to study on your own, but sometimes have typos. Problem sets are helpful, but exams are harder than the practice. He is very kind and genuinely wants to see students succeed.

Took in winter term. Fantastic guy. He genuinely wants to see you do well. IF YOU EVER HAVE A QUESTION IN CLASS DONT BE AFRAID TO ASK! 

If you can get over the 30/70 mt/final or 100 final grade scheme, he is good.

I'd vouch for him and genuinely wish him the best.

Loved 312 with Gherardo-- everything was very straightforward and organized. His slides were helpful, explanations very clear, and tests were fair. He genuinely cares about students. 
Prepare yourself for tests with many practice questions -- the test questions are similar to the practice, but at a higher difficulty. Final has very complicated NPV!

His accent is a bit hard to understand. I love that there's no homework, group projects, textbook, or mandatory attendance. How you do is up to you. But I suggest going to the lectures and practicing. He answers questions and genuinely wants the class to understand. You can't use a financial calculator for exams so prepare for that.

Gherardo is a fantastic lecturer. If you went to class, he was clear about what would be on the tests. He showed genuine concern for his students and allocated additional time for office hours if necessary. He has been one of the best professors I have had so far!

first Beedie professor that seems interested and invested in what they are talking about! so receptive to what students want out of the class and all the content in the class is highly relevant. If you study and attend lecture it shouldn't be difficult to score above the curve.
What a breathe of fresh air.  He was organized, patient, helpful, and very passionate.  One of the best profs yet at Beedie.  Wish him a great career as a lecturer/professor.

He knew his stuff. The 30% midterm 70% final seemed awful, until we did the midterm and it was identical to the simple practice questions he gives. So I thought the final would be around the same level of difficulty, as profs use similar exam styles for midterms and finals. WRONG. Final was so bad with tricks/concepts not covered. Avoid if possible

He is a very caring and organized professor. his slides have all the information you need that will be covered in the exam. even though the weights 30/70 for midterm/final is kinda harsh but make sure you review every single example/question in the problem set that is given, you'll be fine. going to his office hour is helpful but the line is long.

Super fun and nice guy, but honestly, it is not really what I care about when it comes to University. I think he teaches the concepts well, but the test did not reflect the lectures whatsoever. The exam grades you on only a few concepts. I think the grading scheme was extremely stressful for a 30% midterm and a 70% final. Find another professor.

He's a great lecturer with good slides. His weekly questions were very helpful when preparing for the exams as they applied the material to various examples. I dislike that his tests' conceptual questions are based on a couple of big topics, as it doesn't reflect the overall material fairly especially on the final. Make sure you know everything.
Very passionate and engaging lecturer. Very clear on what will be tested and on exam format. Cares about students and always available to answer questions.
Gherardo is definitely one of the best proffs

From what I've seen below, I'd agree and disagree. Midterm was free, final was a more challenging. Prof cares, though lectures are a bit confusing. I took a 100% final and got an A. Everyone saying question 1 was insane but it was a pretty basic concept tbh. Nice guy though, would take again. Studied for the final for 3 days and didn't attend lec.

Haven't really done one of these before. I self studied & probably did well on the exam. However, 312 is a risk with this prof since you're getting marked 100% of your grade on 2 analytical questions and the rest theory. Claim there won't be tricks and the first question is a trick.
Not to mention you don't learn how to use financial calc. AVOID!

Very disorganized, the course lacked structure. There was a syllabus with chapters correlated to the weeks, but the book was not required, so it was irrelevant. The problem sets given did not have questions for the assigned week and the same problem set was used for two or three weeks which was difficult to understand which concepts to work on.

Gherardo is the best professor I've ever had. He has inspired me to study finance at a deeper level. You will earn a good mark if you pay attention and listen to his lectures. 

If I had the option, I would gladly have Gherardo as my professor for the rest of my semesters at SFU.

Gherardo is a very enthusiastic professor you can tell he cares about what he's teaching. However, for a final worth 70% of your grade, you are studying the entire course just for your entire grade to be dependant on 2 calculation questions and some theory. Extremely disappointed as I had a good understanding of the course but the final was rough.

Gherardo genuinely cares about his students and actively seeks feedback on how he could make the course more digestible. He regularly checks in during lectures to ensure everyone understands the material and does not hesitate to re-explain the concept if even one person is confused. Exams were fair if you went to class and did the problem sets.

Gheraldo is a nice person, but does not explain the core concepts properly. His problem sets helps understand the course material. His grading options are either 30% midterm / 70% final or 100% final. His final exam did NOT reflect on the concepts he taught in class, thus making it hard to pass it unless if you have prior knowledge about finance.

Gherardo cares a lot about his students and is very open to feedback about how he lectures; he already said he is looking to rework the course structure based on feedback from this semester. Can't complain about a prof that is so genuinely trying his best. His lectures were very useful and he was always willing to answer questions.
Do-or-die situation during the finals and unfair exam.

The disparity in reviews here is purely if people went to class and paid attention or not. If you went to class it was easy, great prof, good explanation and pace. If not, tough to get organized and understand the many variables and math. Gives good explanations in office hours as well.
Gherardo is a good prf. I agree that the structure of 30/70 is crazy. But in the exam, other than the last part of the first question, the rest was pretty straightforward.
Great professor, fair exams, super good classes.
Class content too disorganized. Midterm was fair but final was not a good reflection of the materials learned

I don't doubt that Gherardo is a good person, he's actually quite friendly. However, this class needs to be reworked. The 30/70 midterm/final split is awful. The midterm was fair for the course content, but the final didn't reflect anything we did in class. I found self studying to be much more helpful than lecture, which is disappointing.

He is a good guy and it is clear he wants to help. He is always available and answers questions quickly. However, the structure of the course and how things are taught leaves something to be desired. A 30/70 exam split is quite intense, and the practice problems do not prepare you well for the final. As well as his slides often times being a mess.
He is a great prof.
Excellent all round prof. Great classes, mega available, very caring. Highly recommended.

This class is quite hard, but Gherardo explained the material perfectly. Also, he really cares about his students: he has done tons of extra office hours during the term, and always responded to emails very very quickly. All in all a great experience.

Very good prof tbh. Clear, great explanations, very organized. Also, for once at SFU I found an almost perfect correspondence between problem sets/ practice questions and what he then asked in the exam.
Pretty glad to have had him this term.

Horrible office hours as he combines both sections causing hour long lineups each week. Does not TAs as they have no clue what's happening. Final exam was awful considering with materials being tested on that are not mentioned in class. Content is disorganized with no proper format.

Pretty good prof tbh. At times, I didn't really understand what I was learning but that's probably more so the nature of the course. The weight of his class is all midterm and final exam based. You have the choice of weighing it 30/70 or 0/100 if you reject your midterm mark. It's pretty scary, but fair enough.

Final was nothing like the problem sets or the midterm. Completely different questions than the problem sets and some questions were extremely wordy. You might think you have an understanding, but then it goes south when you are tested on material you've never seen before. Wish he gave more problem sets on the topics we would see in the final.
Star professor: clear, fun, caring, and very knowledgeable. Overall a great experience.

Thanks to Gherardo i m thinking to concentrate in finance. Incredibly interesting classes, taught perfectly. Lots of practice questions and examples, that give a perfect picture of what you will find in the exam. Top prof.
Simply best prof ever. Teaches well and cares a lot about his students. Also, for the first time I knew exactly what I would have found in the exam.
Amazing prof. Teaches super well, gives a lot of examples and practice questions, and basically says in class what he will ask in the exam. Super recommended

First half of the sem was good. Plenty of practice questions, midterm worth 30%, material in review applied to the midterm. Second half was a mess. 1 PQ/chapter. Canvas SO confusing to follow with multiple topics piled into one. Practice problems and review set were far different than the final (worth 70%). I would avoid this prof if possible.

He teaches well. Midterm was also like the practice and lecture material he taught.
Final was a different story. I don't know if there is a Quota at SFU to make students fail and repeat classes but with professors like Gherardo who make the final exam completely different from anything we have seen in the course, makes me believe that there is.

Great human being: available, nice, considerate, respectful, and knowledgeable. His classes are always useful and interesting. He gives few practice questions, true, but the ones he gives are enough to thoroughly understand the material. In short: best prof ever.

Top notch professor. Great classes, and very useful problem sets. He is also very good at setting expectations: it's very clear what he wants you to know, and very clear about what he is going to ask in exams.

The course is only graded by the midterm and the final. He only provides a few practice questions and nothing more. I've found that some of the problem sets have some questions with material not covered in lecture and the answer sheet shows formulas never used before so you are required to try and decipher it on your own.
Practice questions are not many, but super well designed, helpful, and insightful. I'm really not into finance, but with Gherardo it has been one of my favourite modules ever.

The final is 70% of your mark. There are Very few Practice Questions to prepare. If you want you can drop your midterm mark which will put all the weight on the Final so 100% of your mark! Office Hours are minimal and get ready to remember tons of formulas.
Solid lectures. Gherardo really know his stuff. Final is worth a lot, but he seems to really give the right tools to succeed.
Best prof ever. Nice, available, and knowledgeable. His lectures are helpful and interesting, and he really drop hints on what he will ask in the exams.

This professor is the worst. The grading is 70% based on the final exam and 30% on the midterm. You have the option to drop the midterm, but if you do, your final exam will count as 100. Additionally, he has only one hour of office hours for two sections, which together have nearly 300 students.  Exams are a pain so difficult and hard marking.

Super Prof. I've always been scared of math, but he made everything very understandable, intuitive, and even entertaining. Questions are kept small on purpose, as they seem to be very representative of what he then asks in the test. Putting it simply: so far the best prof I've had at SFU.

You are graded entirely through exams (30% midterm and 70% final). He doesn't allow financial calculators, doesn't provide formulas on exams, and gives very few practice problems after each lecture with no clear exam structure. He is also difficult to reach; there were 20-40 min. long lines to get into his office for his office hours each week.

Gherardo does a great job ! Lots of examples and lots of questions to practice with. His explanations are also very very clear, and he is always happy to give clarifications and explain further. Great experience overall !

Lectures are fine, but he barely gives any questions to practice at home. His problem sets will consist of 3-4 practice questions and that's it. What really gets me is that he doesn't assign specific questions for the week, so some of the problem set answers will not be revealed for 2 weeks which causes you to do your own research. Final is 70%.

I found the content pretty difficult, but Gherardo was a great professor - he knows a lot, and he really tries his best to make everybody understand. He's also very receptive and empathetic - fundamental qualities for a good professor. Would 100% take this again.

Great professor. He really cares about his students, explains clearly, and makes classes fun and entertaining. Only downside is the exam structure: 70% on the final exam is pretty intense. But overall, I'd definitely take it again.
Amazing professor, very very knowledgeable. Explanations are super clear, and he is extremely available, helpful, respectful, and nice. Definitely recommended.

Gherardo is ok if your goal is just to pass this course and get the credit. If you are truly passionate about finance with high expectations to learn, you are better off with YouTube than his lectures. No extra materials other than 4-5 practice questions after each lecture. Often doesn't elaborate on how and why behind key points.
